substances.name: Sublingual/Buccal Estradiol
toxicities: Thromboembolic and cardiovascular risk depends on patient factors, route, formulation, and indication.
Estrogen exposure can affect breast and endometrial tissue; product-specific contraindications and warnings apply.
Hepatic disease and hormone-sensitive malignancy require product-specific clinical review.
substances.summary: 颊含雌二醇 的制剂专属资料条目，重点记录药代时间进程、采样时点和检测方法注意事项。

indications: Approved estrogen indications vary by product
Feminizing hormone therapy guideline or literature context
majorWarnings: Thromboembolic and cardiovascular risk depends on patient factors, route, formulation, and indication.
Estrogen exposure can affect breast and endometrial tissue; product-specific contraindications and warnings apply.
Hepatic disease and hormone-sensitive malignancy require product-specific clinical review.
majorInteractions: Enzyme inducers or inhibitors can alter exposure depending on route and formulation.
Estrogens can alter thyroid-binding globulin and may affect interpretation or requirements of thyroid therapy.
Estrogens can reduce lamotrigine concentrations in some contexts.
monitoring: Estradiol and total testosterone when clinically indicated
Cardiometabolic risk factors when clinically indicated
Prolactin only when symptoms, regimen, or local guidance indicate

sourceRefs.title: Pharmacokinetics of Sublingual Versus Oral Estradiol in Transgender Women

mechanisms: Estrogen receptor alpha and beta agonism
monitoringLabs: Estradiol and total testosterone when clinically indicated
Cardiometabolic risk factors when clinically indicated
Prolactin only when symptoms, regimen, or local guidance indicate
assayCaveats: Sampling time, route, formulation, and time since last dose can materially change measured estradiol.
Immunoassays and LC-MS/MS methods may not agree, particularly at low concentrations or with oral estrogen metabolites.
The laboratory method and local reference interval must be recorded with each result.
safetySignals: Venous thromboembolism and cardiovascular risk context

sourceRefs.title: Pharmacokinetics of Sublingual Versus Oral Estradiol in Transgender Women
Oral estrogen leads to falsely low concentrations of estradiol in a common immunoassay
sourceRefs.note: Assay bias can depend on formulation and assay platform.

notes: Rapid peaks and steep early declines make sampling time especially important.
Some of the dose may be swallowed; exposure varies with technique and formulation.

sourceRefs.title: Pharmacokinetics of Sublingual Versus Oral Estradiol in Transgender Women
Pharmacokinetics after transbuccal administration to postmenopausal women

doseUseReferences.formulation: micronized tablet used sublingually or buccally
doseUseReferences.amountText: 1 mg single sublingual estradiol dose in the cited PK study

sourceRefs.title: Pharmacokinetics of Sublingual Versus Oral Estradiol in Transgender Women

ranges.label: PK study regimen
ranges.note: Rapid peak exposure makes sampling time essential.

caveats: Sampling time, formulation, and assay method strongly affect interpretation.

sourceRefs.title: Oral estrogen leads to falsely low concentrations of estradiol in a common immunoassay
sourceRefs.note: Assay bias can depend on formulation and assay platform.
